rationale: Elec. Code §273.101 governs secretary of state review of home-rule city ballot propositions for charter amendments, initiatives, or referenda. It does not address state-level homestead exemption amounts under Tax Code §11.13(b).
quote: Sec. 273.101. REVIEW BY SECRETARY OF STATE. (a) Not later than the seventh day after the date on which a home-rule city publishes in the election order ballot proposition language proposing an amendment to the city charter or a voter-initiated initiative or referendum as requested by petition, a registered voter eligible to vote in the election or an authorized representative of a home-rule city may submit the proposition for review by the secretary of state.
_note: No conflict; rule pertains to municipal ballot proposition review, not state tax exemption levels.
